textPlain: "On the other hand, if we can’t accept failure in our hearts, we’ve already lost. Not only do we deny ourselves the possibility of succeeding (and what is success in art but a chance to enjoy ourselves and connect with people?), but from experiencing the process and all that it has to teach us. We have to embrace it all or embrace nothing."Words of wisdom and inspiration.

when reading that I couldn't help but think of how you could learn from failure from this early apple story:https://www.folklore.org/Make_a_Mess,_Clean_it_Up!.html(maybe not the same as not worrying about failure)
This is great.  One of the things I say to my wife all the time, which is taken from a Kurt Vonnegut quote, is that "I don't have to be good at my hobbies".
What's the positive power electronics thing? I think I missed it. Would love to see the take.
You could head for Chesterton as well: "anything worth doing is worth doing poorly."
